summary: @variational/common-ui exfiltrates credentials and opens a reverse shell on import
details: On require/import, lib/index.js executes a delayed (0.5–2.5s) malicious side-effect block. It scrapes process.env for credential-shaped keys (matching KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY), reads hostname, username, cwd, id/whoami output, and /proc/1/environ, and POSTs the JSON payload to 2.25.186.116:8443/t. The same block then opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:4444 and pipes a /bin/sh -i interactive shell bidirectionally to the socket (with a python3 socket+subprocess one-liner as a fallback), giving the operator a reverse shell on any machine that imports the package. Module names (http, os, child_process, net), the shell path (/bin/sh), the python3 command, and the destination IP octets are all encoded as charcode arrays passed to String.fromCharCode to evade casual inspection. The advertised exports (ABORT_REASON_*, createPollerConfig) are minimal stubs that exist only to provide cover for the malicious side-effect; the package.json describes itself as 'Shared UI constants and utilities for Variational protocol frontend applications' (homepage variational.io), which is inconsistent with the observed behavior and indicates either a hijack or a namespace-squat impersonating the @variational scope.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
